TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: tiamen a se Payuan nu lima tsaviɫ a maɫevuq.
tiamen a se Payuan nu lima cavilj a maljevuq.
S.TRANSL: We Paiwans hold a festival every five years.

S.FORM: a patagiɫ malang tua qapuḍung katua vuluq.
a patagilj malang tua qapudrung katua vuluq.
S.TRANSL: We begin by making a ball and spears.

S.FORM: qau ita qiɫas a kipalak.
qau ita qiljas a kipalak.
S.TRANSL: We practise for one month.

S.FORM: nu itanga a qiɫas, sema tjua mamazangiɫan a maɫevuq.
nu itanga a qiljas, sema tjua mamazangiljan a maljevuq.
S.TRANSL: After the month we go to the chief to hold the festival,

S.FORM: katsuin a vuluq katua qapuḍung.
kacuin a vuluq katua qapudrung.
S.TRANSL: taking the spears and the ball.

S.FORM: qau seman tsakar i likuz tua tsatsavaɫ.
qau seman cakar i likuz tua cacavalj.
S.TRANSL: We make a ceremonial platform behind the village gate.

S.FORM: pitju a qadaw i likuz tua tsatsavaɫ.
pitju a qadaw i likuz tua cacavalj.
S.TRANSL: It is seven days behind the gate.

S.FORM: qau sema tjai qayaw anga tua tsatsavaɫ, malang tauta tua tsakar.
qau sema tjai qayaw anga tua cacavalj, malang tauta tua cakar.
S.TRANSL: Then we come in front of the gate and make another platform.

S.FORM: tuazua qadaw a vavayan seman qavay seman vava.
tuazua qadaw a vavayan seman qavay seman vava.
S.TRANSL: That day the women make dumplings and beer.

S.FORM: nu tjelu anga a qadaw zemiazian a ḍusa a qadaw.
nu tjelu anga a qadaw zemiazian a drusa a qadaw.
S.TRANSL: Every three days we dance for two days (or After three days we dance for two days).

S.FORM: kenvava, qemapuḍupuḍung anga i tjai qayaw i tsatsavaɫ.
kenvava, qemapudrupudrung anga i tjai qayaw i cacavalj.
S.TRANSL: We drink and play the ball game in front of the gate.

S.FORM: avan anga tuazua qemaqivu tua vuvu.
avan anga tuazua qemaqivu tua vuvu.
S.TRANSL: And that’s when we summon our grandparents,

S.FORM: qau pakan anga tua qavay tua vava.
qau pakan anga tua qavay tua vava.
S.TRANSL: and give them dumplings and beer.

S.FORM: semenay anga mapuɫat.
semenay anga mapuljat.
S.TRANSL: We all sing.

S.FORM: avan a ɫapiɫapi qinaɫan a mamazangiɫan katua puqenetj a tsautsau a sipatjanema a ngadan nu semenay.
avan a ljapiljapi qinaljan a mamazangiljan katua puqenetj a caucau a sipatjanema a ngadan nu semenay.
S.TRANSL: When we sing, it’s the chiefs of neighbouring villages and the famous people whose names we mention.

S.FORM: kemasi tsuay azua a pinatagiɫ.
kemasi cuay azua a pinatagilj.
S.TRANSL: This began long ago.

S.FORM: nu lima a qadaw qematsuvung; vaik a qemaɫup.
nu lima a qadaw qemacuvung; vaik a qemaljup.
S.TRANSL: We finish after five days, and go and hunt.

S.FORM: nu mangtjez a naqemaɫup, a tsuru nua vuluq alangen.
nu mangtjez a naqemaljup, a curu nua vuluq alangen.
S.TRANSL: When we come back from hunting, we make tips for our spears.

S.FORM: qau vaik a sema itjua vutu a maqinatsap.
qau vaik a sema itjua vutu a maqinacap.
S.TRANSL: And we go to enemy areas to hunt heads.

S.FORM: nu qemtsi, katsuin a qulu; qau maleva.
nu qemci, kacuin a qulu; qau maleva.
S.TRANSL: When we kill someone, we bring the head back and celebrate.

S.FORM: nu mangtjez i tjumaq, maqepu; qau seman qaɫa sema tjua mamazangiɫan.
nu mangtjez i tjumaq, maqepu; qau seman qalja sema tjua mamazangiljan.
S.TRANSL: When we get home, people gather, and we celebrate victory over the enemy at the chief’s place.

S.FORM: qau zemian: "nanguaq a tja tsaviɫ."
qau zemian: "nanguaq a tja cavilj."
S.TRANSL: We dance to celebrate: “It’s a good year for us.”

S.FORM: a uqaɫay vavayan kakeḍian mapuɫat maqepu sema tjua mamazangiɫan.
a uqaljay vavayan kakedrian mapuljat maqepu sema tjua mamazangiljan.
S.TRANSL: Men, women and children all gather at the chief’s place.

S.FORM: a naqemtsi a tsautsau kematsu tua ḍiḍi, tu sipakan tua qintsi.
a naqemci a caucau kemacu tua dridri, tu sipakan tua qinci.
S.TRANSL: The person who made the kill brings a pig, to feed the person killed.

S.FORM: qau zemiazian, saka qemauqauqaw a malevaleva tua qintsian.
qau zemiazian, saka qemauqauqaw a malevaleva tua qincian.
S.TRANSL: And we dance, and shout to celebrate the killing.

S.FORM: a qulu nua qintsi pinivetsekadan tua katsasavan a pinivavaw tua valanga.
a qulu nua qinci pinivecekadan tua kacasavan a pinivavaw tua valanga.
S.TRANSL: The head of the person killed is put in the middle of the courtyard on a mortar,

S.FORM: qau uqaɫay vavayan kakeḍian kipalingulinguɫ a zemian.
qau uqaljay vavayan kakedrian kipalingulingulj a zemian.
S.TRANSL: and men, women and children dance round it.

S.FORM: nu lima a qadaw puamin anga; sitjanga qaɫaqaɫa.
nu lima a qadaw puamin anga; sitjanga qaljaqalja.
S.TRANSL: We finish after five days. We have treated the dead like a guest (?).

S.FORM: qau puamin anga.
qau puamin anga.
S.TRANSL: That’s the end.
